Based on the market context (AI infrastructure leading, tech at record highs, neutral-to-risk-on sentiment), here are 4 candidates aligned with the strategy:

**SYMBOL:** NVDA
**THESIS:** Core AI infrastructure play — data center GPU demand remains the primary driver of the current tech rally and is unlikely to decelerate without a macro shock.
**ENTRY_AROUND:** Verify live — confirm price is above 50-day MA and RSI < 70 before entry; MRVL's 30% surge may have pulled NVDA higher and into overbought territory
**STOP_LOSS:** Entry × 0.93
**TARGET:** Entry × 1.15 (scale: 1.15 / 1.18 / 1.20)

## Before Entry — Verify These for Each:

| Check | Why |
|---|---|
| Price > 50-day MA | Strategy entry rule #1 |
| RSI 14 < 70 | Don't chase after yesterday's AI surge |
| No earnings within 3 trading days | Rule #4 — NVDA and ORCL have quarterly reports; confirm dates |
| Volume on entry day | Prefer above-average volume confirmation |

**Key concern today:** After MRVL +30% and HPE +20%, the AI sector may open with RSI readings already stretched. If NVDA or AVGO open above RSI 70, **pass and wait for a pullback day** — the strategy explicitly avoids chasing overbought conditions. Better to miss a move than buy a top.

**MRVL and HPE themselves:** Both are disqualified today — they just ran 20–30%, likely RSI > 80. Don't chase.
